title: Understanding the RACI Chart: Roles and Benefits
summary: An overview of the RACI chart, describing the four roles—Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed—and how the matrix clarifies responsibilities and streamlines project communication.
content: R – Responsible: the person who performs the work. A – Accountable: the person who makes final decisions and signs off. C – Consulted: people whose input is required before work proceeds. I – Informed: people who need to be kept aware of progress. The RACI matrix aligns expectations, reduces miscommunication, and supports clear governance in projects.
can_answer: How does a RACI chart improve project communication?
What are the responsibilities of each RACI role?
